methods.description: Process for collecting and compiling data on bone assemblages from published zooarchaeological reports in the southern Levant, focusing on ungulate species.

steps.description: Data on bone assemblages was collected from all published zooarchaeological reports from the southern Levant which discuss either the presence or the number of identified specimens (NISP) of ungulates.
steps.searchable_summary: collect data on bone assemblages from zooarchaeological reports

steps.description: Compiled information from 382 temporally distinct zooarchaeological assemblages from 190 sites, irrespective of whether they contained wild ungulates or not.
Divided the zooarchaeological database into 12 periods that correspond with temporal boundaries for the established cultural entities.

methods.description: Selection of specific ungulate taxa for analysis based on their identifiability to the species level and exclusion of species that are difficult to distinguish from domestic relatives.

steps.description: Focused on those ungulate taxa which can be identified to the species level and are not at risk of being confused with related domestic species.
steps.searchable_summary: select identifiable ungulate taxa for analysis

methods.description: Statistical analysis using ANOVA and Bonferroni Multiple Comparison Test to compare the weight of extinct species among periods with the weight of surviving species today.

steps.description: One-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) and the Bonferroni Multiple Comparison Test were used to compare the weight (kg) of extinct species among the periods with the weight of the surviving species today.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and Bonferroni test on species weight data
